description: Comprehensive monitoring dashboard for Apache Kafka brokers covering cluster health, throughput, request performance, consumer lag, topic details, JVM resources, network connections, log management, delayed operations, ZooKeeper/KRaft metrics, quotas, and authentication. Compatible with Prometheus JMX Exporter and OpenTelemetry Collector.
title: Kafka Server Monitoring

widgets.description: Number of active controllers in the cluster. Should always be exactly 1.
widgets.title: Active Controller Count

widgets.description: Total number of active Kafka brokers in the cluster.
widgets.title: Total Brokers

widgets.description: Number of under-replicated partitions. Non-zero indicates potential data loss risk.
widgets.title: Under-Replicated Partitions

widgets.description: Number of offline partitions. Any non-zero value means data unavailability.
widgets.title: Offline Partitions Count

widgets.description: Total number of partitions across all brokers.
widgets.title: Total Partitions

widgets.description: Total number of partition leaders across all brokers.
widgets.title: Leader Count

widgets.description: Number of partitions where the preferred replica is not the leader. High values indicate leader imbalance.
widgets.title: Preferred Replica Imbalance

widgets.description: Global partition count as seen by the controller.
widgets.title: Global Partition Count

widgets.description: Rate at which ISR (In-Sync Replica) set is shrinking. Sustained shrinks indicate broker issues.
widgets.title: ISR Shrink Rate

widgets.description: Rate at which ISR set is expanding. Should increase after a shrink event as replicas catch up.
widgets.title: ISR Expand Rate

widgets.description: Rate of leader elections. Frequent elections indicate instability.
widgets.title: Leader Election Rate

widgets.description: Rate of unclean leader elections. These can cause data loss. Should always be 0.
widgets.title: Unclean Leader Elections

widgets.description: Leader partition distribution across brokers. Should be roughly even.
widgets.title: Leader Count by Broker

widgets.description: Partition distribution across brokers. Should be roughly even.
widgets.title: Partition Count by Broker

widgets.description: Rate of incoming messages per second by topic.
widgets.title: Messages In Per Second

widgets.description: Rate of bytes received per second by topic.
widgets.title: Bytes In Per Second

widgets.description: Rate of bytes sent per second by topic.
widgets.title: Bytes Out Per Second

widgets.description: Message rate per broker. Helps identify hot brokers.
widgets.title: Messages In Per Broker

widgets.description: Bytes in rate per broker.
widgets.title: Bytes In Per Broker

widgets.description: Total produce request rate per broker.
widgets.title: Total Produce Requests/sec

widgets.description: Total fetch request rate per broker.
widgets.title: Total Fetch Requests/sec

widgets.description: Rate of failed produce requests. Non-zero values indicate client or broker issues.
widgets.title: Failed Produce Requests/sec

widgets.description: Rate of failed fetch requests. Non-zero values indicate consumer or broker issues.
widgets.title: Failed Fetch Requests/sec

widgets.description: Rate of bytes rejected due to message validation or quota violations.
widgets.title: Bytes Rejected Per Second

widgets.description: Rate of replication bytes received. Indicates inter-broker replication traffic.
widgets.title: Replication Bytes In Per Second

widgets.description: Rate of replication bytes sent to followers.
widgets.title: Replication Bytes Out Per Second

widgets.description: Rate of message format conversions during fetch. High values indicate mixed client versions causing CPU overhead.
widgets.title: Fetch Message Conversions/sec

widgets.description: Average idle percentage of request handler threads. Below 0.3 (30%) is concerning, below 0.1 is critical.
widgets.title: Request Handler Avg Idle %

widgets.description: Average idle percentage of network processor threads. Below 0.3 is concerning.
widgets.title: Network Processor Avg Idle %

widgets.description: Total time for Produce requests including queue, local, remote, and send time.
widgets.title: Produce Request Total Time (Mean)

widgets.description: Total time for FetchConsumer requests.
widgets.title: FetchConsumer Request Total Time (Mean)

widgets.description: Total time for FetchFollower requests (replication).
widgets.title: FetchFollower Request Total Time (Mean)

widgets.description: Time requests spend waiting in the request queue. High values indicate overloaded request handlers.
widgets.title: Request Queue Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Queue time for FetchConsumer requests.
widgets.title: Request Queue Time (FetchConsumer)

widgets.description: Queue time for FetchFollower requests.
widgets.title: Request Queue Time (FetchFollower)

widgets.description: Time responses spend in the response queue.
widgets.title: Response Queue Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Response queue time for FetchConsumer requests.
widgets.title: Response Queue Time (FetchConsumer)

widgets.description: Response queue time for FetchFollower requests.
widgets.title: Response Queue Time (FetchFollower)

widgets.description: Time to send response back to the client for Produce requests.
widgets.title: Response Send Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Response send time for FetchConsumer.
widgets.title: Response Send Time (FetchConsumer)

widgets.description: Response send time for FetchFollower.
widgets.title: Response Send Time (FetchFollower)

widgets.description: Time spent processing request locally (leader processing time).
widgets.title: Local Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Time waiting for followers to replicate (acks=all). High values indicate slow followers.
widgets.title: Remote Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Local processing time for consumer fetch requests.
widgets.title: Local Time (FetchConsumer)

widgets.description: Remote wait time for consumer fetch requests. High values may indicate fetch.wait.max.ms settings.
widgets.title: Remote Time (FetchConsumer)

widgets.description: Overall request rate broken down by request type.
widgets.title: Request Rate by Type

widgets.description: Rate of request errors by type. Should be close to zero.
widgets.title: Request Error Rate

widgets.description: Consumer group lag per partition. High lag means consumers are falling behind producers.
widgets.title: Consumer Group Lag by Topic

widgets.description: Total consumer lag aggregated across all partitions per consumer group.
widgets.title: Consumer Group Lag Sum by Group

widgets.description: Number of members in each consumer group. Drops indicate rebalancing or consumer failures.
widgets.title: Consumer Group Members

widgets.description: Current committed offset for each consumer group's topic-partitions.
widgets.title: Current Offset by Topic-Partition

widgets.description: Rate at which consumer group offsets are advancing. Flat lines indicate stalled consumers.
widgets.title: Consumer Offset Commit Rate

widgets.description: Rate of change in consumer lag. Positive = lag increasing (bad), Negative = lag decreasing (catching up).
widgets.title: Consumer Group Lag Rate of Change

widgets.description: Consumer group state and member count. Monitor for frequent state transitions.
widgets.title: Consumer Group State

widgets.description: Top 10 topics by message rate.
widgets.title: Messages In by Topic (Top 10)

widgets.description: Top 10 topics by bytes in rate.
widgets.title: Bytes In by Topic (Top 10)

widgets.description: Top 10 topics by bytes out rate.
widgets.title: Bytes Out by Topic (Top 10)

widgets.description: Log end offset for each topic-partition. Growth rate indicates production rate.
widgets.title: Log End Offset by Topic

widgets.description: Total log size per topic on disk.
widgets.title: Log Size by Topic

widgets.description: Log start offset per topic-partition. Increases indicate log retention cleanup.
widgets.title: Log Start Offset by Topic

widgets.description: Number of log segments per topic. Many segments may indicate frequent rolls.
widgets.title: Log Segments Count

widgets.description: Rate of offset advancement per topic, showing production velocity.
widgets.title: Topic Partition Offset Rate

widgets.description: Bytes rejected per topic due to message format errors or quota violations.
widgets.title: Topic Bytes Rejected

widgets.description: JVM heap memory usage per broker. Monitor for memory pressure.
widgets.title: JVM Heap Memory Used

widgets.description: JVM non-heap memory (Metaspace, code cache, etc.) per broker.
widgets.title: JVM Non-Heap Memory Used

widgets.description: Maximum configured heap size per broker.
widgets.title: JVM Heap Memory Max

widgets.description: JVM committed heap memory per broker.
widgets.title: JVM Heap Memory Committed

widgets.description: Rate of time spent in garbage collection. High values cause latency spikes.
widgets.title: GC Collection Time Rate

widgets.description: Rate of GC collections. Frequent minor GCs are normal; frequent major GCs indicate memory issues.
widgets.title: GC Collection Count Rate

widgets.description: Individual JVM memory pool usage (Eden, Survivor, Old Gen, etc.).
widgets.title: JVM Memory Pool Usage

widgets.description: JVM buffer pool usage (direct and mapped buffers). Important for Kafka's zero-copy performance.
widgets.title: JVM Buffer Pool Used

widgets.description: Current JVM thread count per broker.
widgets.title: Thread Count

widgets.description: JVM daemon thread count per broker.
widgets.title: Daemon Thread Count

widgets.description: JVM peak thread count per broker. Shows historical thread count high watermark.
widgets.title: Peak Thread Count

widgets.description: Number of open file descriptors per broker. Kafka uses many FDs for log segments and connections.
widgets.title: Open File Descriptors

widgets.description: Maximum file descriptors limit. Monitor open/max ratio to avoid FD exhaustion.
widgets.title: Max File Descriptors

widgets.description: Broker CPU utilization. Rate of CPU seconds consumed.
widgets.title: Process CPU Usage

widgets.description: Process resident set size (RSS). The actual physical memory used by the broker process.
widgets.title: Process Resident Memory

widgets.description: Process virtual memory size per broker.
widgets.title: Process Virtual Memory

widgets.description: Broker process start time. Useful for detecting unexpected restarts.
widgets.title: Process Start Time

widgets.description: Number of active connections per broker and listener.
widgets.title: Active Connections

widgets.description: Rate of new connection creation. Spikes indicate client reconnection storms.
widgets.title: Connection Creation Rate

widgets.description: Rate of connection closures per broker.
widgets.title: Connection Close Rate

widgets.description: Rate of connections killed due to idle timeout.
widgets.title: Expired Connections Killed

widgets.description: Raw incoming byte rate at the network level per broker.
widgets.title: Incoming Byte Rate

widgets.description: Raw outgoing byte rate at the network level per broker.
widgets.title: Outgoing Byte Rate

widgets.description: Request byte count rate by request type.
widgets.title: Request Size (Avg)

widgets.description: Size of the request queue in the network layer. Growing queue indicates broker overload.
widgets.title: Network Request Queue Size

widgets.description: Size of the response queue. Growing response queue indicates slow network send.
widgets.title: Response Queue Size

widgets.description: Request handler pool idle percent. Low idle = high IO wait / overloaded handlers.
widgets.title: IO Wait Time (Produce)

widgets.description: Rate of log flushes to disk per broker.
widgets.title: Log Flush Rate

widgets.description: Log flush latency in milliseconds. High values indicate slow disk I/O.
widgets.title: Log Flush Latency (ms)

widgets.description: Percentage of log that needs to be recopied during compaction. High values indicate compaction inefficiency.
widgets.title: Log Cleaner Recopy Percent

widgets.description: Maximum time spent on a single log compaction run.
widgets.title: Log Cleaner Max Clean Time

widgets.description: Log cleaner deduplication buffer utilization. High values may require more buffer allocation.
widgets.title: Log Cleaner Max Buffer Utilization

widgets.description: Maximum dirty ratio across all compactable logs. High values indicate compaction falling behind.
widgets.title: Log Cleaner Dead Ratio

widgets.description: Number of produce requests waiting in purgatory for replication acknowledgement.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - Produce

widgets.description: Number of fetch requests waiting in purgatory for enough data to satisfy fetch.min.bytes.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - Fetch

widgets.description: Number of DeleteRecords operations waiting in purgatory.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - DeleteRecords

widgets.description: Number of leader election operations waiting in purgatory.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - ElectLeader

widgets.description: Number of rebalance operations waiting in purgatory.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - Rebalance

widgets.description: All delayed operation purgatory sizes aggregated.
widgets.title: Purgatory Size - topic

widgets.description: Number of delayed produce operations. High numbers indicate slow replication.
widgets.title: Delayed Produce Expires/sec

widgets.description: Number of delayed fetch operations.
widgets.title: Delayed Fetch Expires/sec

widgets.description: Average ZooKeeper request latency from Kafka brokers. High latency indicates ZK overload or network issues.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Request Latency (Avg)

widgets.description: Rate of ZooKeeper session expiry events. Any non-zero rate is concerning.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Session Expiry Rate

widgets.description: Rate of ZooKeeper disconnection events.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Disconnect Rate

widgets.description: Rate of ZooKeeper sync reconnection events.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Sync Connect Rate

widgets.description: Rate of ZooKeeper authentication failures. Should always be 0.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Auth Failures Rate

widgets.description: Rate of ZooKeeper read-only connections. Indicates ZK quorum loss.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper Read-Only Connects

widgets.description: Rate of SASL authentication events with ZooKeeper.
widgets.title: ZooKeeper SASL Authentications

widgets.description: Current KRaft controller leader ID.
widgets.title: KRaft Current Leader

widgets.description: Average commit latency in KRaft consensus.
widgets.title: KRaft Commit Latency

widgets.description: KRaft metadata log end offset.
widgets.title: KRaft Log End Offset

widgets.description: KRaft applied metadata offset. Lag between log end and applied offset indicates slow controller.
widgets.title: KRaft Applied Offset

widgets.description: Rate of metadata records fetched by KRaft followers.
widgets.title: KRaft Fetch Records Rate

widgets.description: Average KRaft election latency.
widgets.title: KRaft Election Latency

widgets.description: Average throttle time applied to producers exceeding their quota.
widgets.title: Produce Throttle Time (Avg)

widgets.description: Average throttle time applied to consumers exceeding their quota.
widgets.title: Fetch Throttle Time (Avg)

widgets.description: Average throttle time applied to clients exceeding request rate quota.
widgets.title: Request Throttle Time (Avg)

widgets.description: Rate of throttle events by quota type.
widgets.title: Throttle Queue Size

widgets.description: Rate of successful authentications by mechanism (PLAIN, SCRAM, etc.).
widgets.title: Authentication Success Rate

widgets.description: Rate of failed authentications. Spikes may indicate brute-force attempts or misconfigured clients.
widgets.title: Authentication Failure Rate

widgets.description: Rate of failed reauthentication attempts.
widgets.title: Failed Reauthentication Rate

widgets.description: Rate of successful reauthentication events.
widgets.title: Successful Reauthentication Rate
